ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_00002694 covers churro as a food item. This record is a multi-item combo (4 churros + 4 drinks); closest_parent used as no combo-meal code exists in FoodOn.
ontology.foodex2_description: Doughnuts and similar fried dough products
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP covers fried dough products. Churros are a fried dough pastry; this is the closest defensible parent. Combo nature (with drinks) not representable in FoodEx2 at item level.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for churros or this combo meal type.

main_ingredients: beverage (type unspecified — likely soft drink or hot chocolate)

serving.description: 1 combo set for 4 persons (4 churros portions + 4 drinks)
serving.natural_language_portion: One combo set for four people, comprising four individual churros servings (approximately 100 g each) and four drinks, totalling approximately 800 g of solid food component.

nutrient_source.title: Churros, plain (fried dough pastry) — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy / Foundation Foods composite
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for 'Churros Republic Rockstar Combo for 4' exists in any approved primary source. Nutrient values are derived from USDA FoodData Central data for plain churros (fried dough pastry, sugar-coated). The beverage component is unspecified in the menu description; nutrient values reflect the churros solid component only, normalised per 100 g. The combo nature (4 churros + 4 drinks) is reflected in the serving size but per-100g values represent the churros pastry. This is an adapted/estimated record. Confidence is medium.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of fried dough / doughnut-type products — analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for fried wheat-flour dough products
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for churros exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 74 is estimated by analogy with fried wheat-flour dough products (doughnuts, fried dough) listed in the University of Sydney GI Database, which report GI values in the range 68–76 (glucose reference = 100). Churros share the same base composition (refined wheat flour, deep-fried, sugar-coated) and are expected to have a similar high GI. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (74 × 49.5 g per 100 g × 0.800 kg serving solid component) / 100 ≈ 36.6 g GL for the full 800 g solid serving.

hypertension.guidance: Each 100 g of churros provides approximately 280 mg sodium; individuals with hypertension should limit portion size and avoid pairing with high-sodium beverages such as sweetened sodas, as cumulative sodium intake across the combo may approach or exceed 400–500 mg per person.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (estimated GI 74) and high refined carbohydrate load
type2_diabetes.guidance: Churros are made from refined wheat flour, deep-fried and sugar-coated, yielding a high estimated GI of 74 and a substantial glycemic load; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit this food, as a single serving per person (~200 g churros) delivers approximately 99 g of carbohydrate with rapid postprandial glucose elevation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat from deep-frying and significant energy density
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: At approximately 19 g fat per 100 g (predominantly from deep-frying in vegetable oil), churros contribute substantially to daily fat intake; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption of deep-fried, energy-dense foods of this type, particularly when consumed as a large sharing combo.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Churros and Drinks Combo for Four (marketed as 'Rockstar Combo for 4' at Churros Republic, Funan, Singapore) is a Spanish-inspired sharing set comprising four individual portions of churros and four accompanying beverages. Churros are a traditional Spanish fried-dough pastry made from a simple choux-style dough of wheat flour, water, and salt, piped through a star-shaped nozzle and deep-fried until golden and crisp on the outside, with a soft, slightly chewy interior. They are typically finished with a coating of cinnamon sugar and served with a dipping sauce such as chocolate or caramel. In Singapore, churros have become a popular dessert and snack item, served at specialty cafes and dessert stalls. Nutritionally, churros are energy-dense (approximately 387 kcal per 100 g), high in refined carbohydrates (~49.5 g per 100 g), and moderately high in fat (~19 g per 100 g) due to deep-frying. The estimated glycemic index is high (~74), making this food unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or metabolic syndrome without strict portion control. The sodium content (~280 mg per 100 g) is moderate and relevant for individuals with hypertension. The beverage component of this combo is unspecified in the menu listing and may include soft drinks, hot chocolate, or other sweetened beverages, which would further increase the sugar and caloric load per person. This combo is intended as a shared dessert experience for four people, with each person receiving one churros portion and one drink.
common_user_queries: What is the GI of churros?

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry for this specific combo product exists in any approved primary source. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central data for plain fried-dough churros. The beverage component is unspecified and excluded from per-100g calculations. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database entries for fried wheat-flour dough products. Confidence is medium due to the adapted/estimated nature of both nutrient and GI data.
notes: 1. This record covers the churros solid component of the combo. The beverage type is not specified in the field survey listing; beverage nutrients are not included in per_100g values. 2. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central data for vegetable-oil-fried dough products (no partially hydrogenated oils reported). 3. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central; traditional churros dough contains no eggs or dairy in the base recipe. 4. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 — naturally zero as churros contain no fruit or vegetable components. 5. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central source entry used. 6. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted USDA source entry. 7. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted USDA source entry; churros contain no animal products in the base recipe. 8. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted USDA source entry. 9. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted USDA source entry. 10. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted USDA source entry. 11. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted USDA source entry. 12. GI of 74 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for churros was found in the University of Sydney GI Database as of the accessed date. 13. The food_name has been normalised from the promotional menu name '🔥Rockstar Combo for 4!🔥' to the canonical English name 'Churros and Drinks Combo for Four'. 14. Halal status is listed as true based on Churros Republic Singapore's publicly stated halal certification; this should be verified against current MUIS records. 15. Serving size of 800 g represents the solid churros component only (4 × ~200 g per person portion); actual total combo weight including beverages would be higher.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Normalised food_name from promotional emoji menu name to canonical English. Added all mandatory multilingual local_names (Chinese simplified/traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi). Built comprehensive aliases and aliases_by_language with full consistency check. Sourced nutrient values from USDA FoodData Central adapted data for churros. Documented all -1 sentinel values with explicit reasons in notes. Set GI to 74 (analogous estimate, University of Sydney GI Database) with full documentation. Populated all three health_context conditions with specific clinical guidance. Wrote LLM description exceeding 200 characters. Verified aliases_by_language to flat aliases consistency.

Iteration 2: Verified zero-macro justification rule compliance — no core macros are zero; trans_fat_g and cholesterol_mg are 0.0 with confirmed-zero justification in notes. Verified all -1 values documented in notes per g3 sentinel rule. Verified ontology match_type values are only 'exact', 'closest_parent', or 'none'. Final self-score confirmed 8/8 with all hard gates passing.
